context: Implementation of Issue #744 (per-split HistoryPane with per-cliToolId filtering) is complete and all checks pass (tsc/lint/test:unit 6738 pass/build). The acceptance-test agent found low-severity quality issues to address before finalizing.
improvement_goals: FINDING-1 (fix): When HistoryPane is rendered inside a split, the collapse button's hardcoded data-testid (e.g. 'history-pane-collapse-button') and aria-controls='worktree-history-pane' (HISTORY_PANE_ID) are duplicated across splits and dangle (HISTORY_PANE_ID is not rendered on PC). Make these per-split unique (suffix by splitIndex) or omit aria-controls when there is no corresponding pane id. Ensure no duplicate DOM ids / data-testids across simultaneously-rendered splits. Keep backward compatibility for the legacy (no splitIndex) usage and mobile.

FINDING-3 (assess only, conservative): On PC the parent pollData still calls fetchMessages() to populate state.messages which is now only consumed by the mobile path; the active CLI is thus fetched twice (parent + split). Assess whether the parent /messages poll can be safely gated to mobile/when needed WITHOUT breaking mobile, handleMessageSent, or other state.messages consumers. ONLY apply if clearly safe and well-tested; otherwise document as a deferred follow-up. Do NOT introduce regressions.

General: ensure useSplitMessages and the per-split wiring follow the same patterns/naming as useTerminalPanePolling; remove any dead code (e.g. leftover historyPaneMemo comments, unused imports); confirm JSDoc/comments reference Issue #744.
constraints: All changes must keep tsc/lint/test:unit/build green
Additive/backward-compatible: legacy HistoryPane usage (no splitIndex) and the mobile path must be unchanged in behavior
Update/extend tests for any DOM id/testid changes so the per-split uniqueness is locked by a test
Do NOT commit; leave changes in working tree
PC-only UI change; do not alter mobile rendering
